question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper mentions that genotypic resistance testing was performed on individuals with a viral load >400 RNA copies/ml, and it describes the sequencing of HIV-1 from plasma samples.

question: From which countries were the sequenced samples obtained?
explain: The study was conducted in Soweto, South Africa, and the samples were obtained from patients at the Chris Hani Baragwanath Hospital.

question: Which drug classes were received by individuals in the study before sample sequencing?
answer: A. NRTI, B. NNRTI, C. PI
explain: The paper mentions that the majority of patients were on an NNRTI-based (first-line) regimen, and a smaller proportion were on a PI-based (second-line) regimen. It also lists specific NRTI and NNRTI mutations.
sentences: "The majority (89%) were on an NNRTI--based (first-line) and 12% were on a PI-based (second-line)."; "Of the 94 viremic patients on the first-line regimen, 64% (n=60), 81% (n=76), and 2% (n=2) had evidence of NRTI, NNRTI, and PI resistance respectively."; "Among the 35 viremic patients failing a second-line regimen, 29% (n=10), 54% (n=19) and 6% (n=2) patients had mutations associated with NRTI resistance (mainly M184V/I, n=9), NNRTI resistance (mainly K103N, n=15), and major PI mutations respectively."
